meta.title: Top 100 Neurosurgery Resources for Surgical Case Preparation

resources.title: Cranial Anatomy and Surgical Approaches
resources.author: Albert L. Rhoton Jr.
resources.description: The definitive atlas of cranial microsurgical anatomy. Essential for understanding every cranial approach, vascular relationships, and cranial nerve anatomy from the surgical perspective.
resources.why_essential: Gold standard for surgical anatomy. Every approach chapter should reference Rhoton.

resources.title: Microneurosurgery (Volumes I-IV)
resources.author: M. Gazi Yasargil
resources.description: Foundational 4-volume work on microsurgical technique. Vol I: Microsurgical Anatomy. Vol II: Clinical Considerations/Surgery of Intracranial Aneurysms and Results. Vol IIIA: AVM of the Brain. Vol IVB: Microneurosurgery of CNS Tumors.
resources.why_essential: Yasargil pioneered modern microsurgery. His technique descriptions remain the benchmark.

resources.title: Handbook of Neurosurgery
resources.author: Mark S. Greenberg
resources.description: Comprehensive single-volume reference covering all of neurosurgery. Quick reference for clinical decision-making, grading scales, dosing, and management algorithms.
resources.why_essential: The 'bible' of neurosurgery for quick reference and board prep.

resources.title: Youmans & Winn Neurological Surgery
resources.author: H. Richard Winn (ed.)
resources.description: Multi-volume comprehensive neurosurgery reference. Covers basic science, clinical evaluation, and all operative subspecialties in depth.
resources.why_essential: Most comprehensive multi-author reference in the field.

resources.title: Schmidek & Sweet: Operative Neurosurgical Techniques
resources.author: Alfredo Quinones-Hinojosa (ed.)
resources.description: Definitive operative techniques reference with step-by-step surgical descriptions, indications, and outcomes for virtually every neurosurgical procedure.
resources.why_essential: Go-to for step-by-step operative technique with detailed illustrations.

resources.title: Operative Neurosurgery (2 Volumes)
resources.author: John E. Kempe
resources.description: Classic operative atlas with detailed surgical illustrations of cranial and spinal procedures.
resources.why_essential: Beautifully illustrated classic operative reference.

resources.title: Atlas of Neurosurgical Techniques (2 Volumes)
resources.author: Laligam N. Sekhar, Richard G. Fessler
resources.description: Vol 1: Brain. Vol 2: Spine and Peripheral Nerves. Comprehensive atlas with modern techniques and step-by-step approaches.
resources.why_essential: Modern comprehensive atlas covering brain, spine, and peripheral nerve surgery.

resources.title: Seven Aneurysms: Tenets and Techniques for Clipping
resources.author: Michael T. Lawton
resources.description: Expert guide to aneurysm clipping organized by aneurysm morphology and location. Includes decision-making frameworks and clip selection strategies.
resources.why_essential: The modern definitive text on open aneurysm surgery from the master.

resources.title: Seven AVMs: Tenets and Techniques for Resection
resources.author: Michael T. Lawton
resources.description: Companion to Seven Aneurysms focused on AVM resection. Organized by AVM location and angioarchitecture.
resources.why_essential: Definitive modern guide to AVM surgery.

resources.title: Meningiomas
resources.author: Ossama Al-Mefty
resources.description: Comprehensive reference on meningioma surgery including skull base meningiomas, with detailed approach-specific chapters.
resources.why_essential: The authoritative text on meningioma management and surgical technique.

resources.title: Neurovascular Surgery
resources.author: Robert F. Spetzler, M. Yashar S. Kalani, Peter Nakaji
resources.description: Comprehensive cerebrovascular surgery text covering aneurysms, AVMs, cavernous malformations, moyamoya, and bypass surgery.
resources.why_essential: Barrow Neurological Institute's cerebrovascular expertise in one volume.

resources.title: Surgery of the Third Ventricle
resources.author: Michael L.J. Apuzzo
resources.description: Detailed text on surgical approaches to the third ventricle including transcallosal, transcortical, and endoscopic approaches.
resources.why_essential: Essential for deep midline lesion surgery.

resources.title: Fundamentals of Operative Techniques in Neurosurgery
resources.author: E. Sander Connolly Jr., Guy M. McKhann II, et al.
resources.description: Practical operative guide with emphasis on technique fundamentals, patient positioning, and step-by-step procedures.
resources.why_essential: Excellent for junior residents learning operative fundamentals.

resources.title: Surgery of the Cerebellopontine Angle
resources.author: Nicholas C. Bambakidis, Cliff A. Megerian, Robert F. Spetzler
resources.description: Focused text on CPA surgery including retrosigmoid, translabyrinthine, and middle fossa approaches.
resources.why_essential: Comprehensive guide to one of the most demanding anatomical regions.

resources.title: Principles of Neurosurgery
resources.author: Setti S. Rengachary, Richard G. Ellenbogen
resources.description: Well-organized principles-based neurosurgery text covering fundamental concepts and clinical practice.
resources.why_essential: Strong foundational text for understanding principles behind techniques.

resources.title: Spine Surgery: Techniques, Complication Avoidance, and Management
resources.author: Edward C. Benzel
resources.description: Comprehensive spine surgery reference emphasizing biomechanics, technique, and avoiding complications.
resources.why_essential: The go-to spine reference with excellent biomechanics foundation.

resources.title: Spine Surgery: Operative Techniques
resources.author: Alexander R. Vaccaro (ed.)
resources.description: Step-by-step spine operative techniques with modern approaches including minimally invasive techniques.
resources.why_essential: Comprehensive modern spine operative atlas.

resources.title: Keyhole Approaches in Neurosurgery
resources.author: Axel Perneczky, Robert Reisch
resources.description: Detailed guide to minimally invasive keyhole craniotomy approaches including supraorbital, retrosigmoid, and supracerebellar.
resources.why_essential: Pioneer text on minimally invasive cranial approaches.

resources.title: Surgical Anatomy of the Brain
resources.author: Guilherme Carvalhal Ribas
resources.description: Surgical anatomy text integrating sulcal-gyral anatomy with surgical approaches and cortical mapping.
resources.why_essential: Bridges surface anatomy with functional localization for surgical planning.

resources.title: Endoscopic Endonasal Transsphenoidal Surgery
resources.author: Amin B. Kassam, Paolo Cappabianca, et al.
resources.description: Comprehensive guide to endoscopic skull base surgery including extended approaches to anterior, middle, and posterior skull base.
resources.why_essential: Essential for the expanding role of endoscopic skull base surgery.

resources.title: Atlas of Human Anatomy
resources.author: Frank H. Netter
resources.description: Classic illustrated anatomy atlas with detailed neuroanatomy sections covering brain, spine, cranial nerves, and vasculature.
resources.why_essential: Gold standard general anatomy reference for quick visual review.

resources.title: Rhoton Photographic Collection
resources.author: Albert L. Rhoton Jr. / AANS
resources.description: Comprehensive photographic collection of microsurgical anatomy dissections covering every region of the brain, skull base, and posterior fossa.
resources.why_essential: Unparalleled photographic documentation of surgical anatomy from the master anatomist.

resources.title: Neuroanatomy: An Atlas of Structures, Sections, and Systems
resources.author: Duane E. Haines
resources.description: Detailed neuroanatomy atlas with cross-sectional anatomy, vascular territories, and clinical correlations.
resources.why_essential: Best atlas for understanding cross-sectional and tract anatomy.

resources.title: The Human Brain: Surface, Three-Dimensional Sectional Anatomy with MRI, and Blood Supply
resources.author: Henri M. Duvernoy
resources.description: Detailed 3D brain anatomy with MRI correlation and comprehensive vascular supply mapping.
resources.why_essential: Critical for correlating surgical anatomy with imaging.

resources.title: The Human Brain Stem and Cerebellum
resources.author: Henri M. Duvernoy
resources.description: Companion atlas focused on posterior fossa anatomy including detailed brainstem nuclear anatomy and cerebellar anatomy.
resources.why_essential: Essential for posterior fossa surgery planning.

resources.title: Neuroanatomy through Clinical Cases
resources.author: Hal Blumenfeld
resources.description: Case-based neuroanatomy integrating structure with function and clinical presentation.
resources.why_essential: Best resource for understanding functional significance of surgical anatomy.

resources.title: Clinical Neuroanatomy
resources.author: Stephen G. Waxman
resources.description: Concise clinical neuroanatomy text with emphasis on clinically relevant anatomy and localization.
resources.why_essential: Quick, clinically-oriented neuroanatomy review.

resources.title: Comprehensive Neurosurgery Board Review
resources.author: Jonathan Stuart Citow, Robert L. Macdonald, Remi Bhardwaj Refai
resources.description: Board review resource covering anatomy, pathology, and surgical concepts in question format.
resources.why_essential: Structured review for confirming anatomical knowledge.

resources.title: Microsurgical Anatomy of the Brain (Rhoton's Cranial Anatomy & Surgical Approaches — Atlas)
resources.author: Albert L. Rhoton Jr.
resources.description: Focused atlas of microsurgical brain anatomy from the surgeon's operating perspective.
resources.why_essential: Surgical perspective anatomy that directly translates to the operating room.

resources.title: Tubbs' Surgical Anatomy of Nerves
resources.author: R. Shane Tubbs, E. Rizk, et al.
resources.description: Detailed surgical anatomy of peripheral and cranial nerves with clinical correlations.
resources.why_essential: Essential reference for nerve-related surgery and avoiding nerve injury.

resources.title: Neurosurgical Atlas (neurosurgicalatlas.com)
resources.author: Aaron Cohen-Gadol
resources.description: Free comprehensive online neurosurgical atlas with operative videos, 3D anatomy, approach descriptions, and surgical pearls for virtually every neurosurgical procedure.
resources.why_essential: The single best free online resource. Operative videos and 3D models for every major approach.

resources.title: The Rhoton Collection
resources.author: AANS / Rhoton Lab
resources.description: Digital archive of Rhoton's microsurgical anatomy dissections, photographs, and teaching materials.
resources.why_essential: Digital access to Rhoton's unparalleled anatomy collection.

resources.title: Radiopaedia.org
resources.author: Radiopaedia Team
resources.description: Free radiology reference with extensive neuroimaging cases, anatomy illustrations, and pathology descriptions.
resources.why_essential: Best free resource for imaging anatomy and pathology correlation.

resources.title: 3D4Medical / Complete Anatomy
resources.author: Elsevier
resources.description: Interactive 3D anatomy application with dissectable layers, clinical correlations, and neurosurgery-specific content.
resources.why_essential: Best interactive 3D anatomy tool for understanding spatial relationships.

resources.title: AANS Neurosurgeon
resources.author: American Association of Neurological Surgeons
resources.description: AANS educational content including case studies, technique articles, and practice guidelines.
resources.why_essential: Authoritative professional society educational resources.

resources.title: Congress of Neurological Surgeons Education Portal
resources.author: CNS
resources.description: CNS educational modules, webinars, operative video library, and guidelines.
resources.why_essential: High-quality structured educational content from CNS.

resources.title: Neurosurgical Atlas YouTube Channel
resources.author: Aaron Cohen-Gadol
resources.description: Extensive library of narrated operative neurosurgery videos covering approaches, techniques, and anatomy.
resources.why_essential: Free high-quality operative videos with expert narration.

resources.title: Stanford Neurosurgery YouTube
resources.author: Stanford Neurosurgery
resources.description: Academic neurosurgery video content including grand rounds, operative cases, and educational lectures.
resources.why_essential: Academic-quality educational content from a top program.

resources.title: Surgical Neurology International (SNI)
resources.author: SNI Editorial Board
resources.description: Open-access neurosurgery journal with technique articles, case reports, and reviews.
resources.why_essential: Free open-access journal with practical technique articles.

resources.title: WFNS Educational Resources
resources.author: World Federation of Neurosurgical Societies
resources.description: International neurosurgery educational materials, consensus guidelines, and training resources.
resources.why_essential: Global perspective on neurosurgical standards and practice.

resources.title: Pterional (Frontotemporal) Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple (Yasargil, Rhoton, Cohen-Gadol)
resources.description: The workhorse cranial approach. Access to anterior and middle cranial fossa, circle of Willis, sellar/parasellar region, and lateral skull base.
resources.positioning: Supine, head rotated 30-45 degrees contralateral, slightly extended, vertex tilted down
resources.why_essential: Most commonly used cranial approach — must master completely.

resources.title: Retrosigmoid (Retromastoid) Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple (Samii, Rhoton, Sekhar)
resources.description: Posterior fossa approach for CPA lesions, petroclival tumors, and microvascular decompression.
resources.positioning: Lateral/park bench or supine with head turned, mastoid highest point
resources.why_essential: Primary approach for CPA pathology and MVD.

resources.title: Suboccipital Midline Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Posterior fossa midline approach for cerebellar and fourth ventricle pathology.
resources.positioning: Prone or sitting (Concorde position), neck flexed
resources.why_essential: Essential approach for midline posterior fossa pathology.

resources.title: Supracerebellar Infratentorial Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Yasargil, Sekhar, Rhoton)
resources.description: Approach to pineal region, posterior third ventricle, and superior cerebellar surface via the natural corridor above the cerebellum.
resources.positioning: Sitting, Concorde, or prone with head flexed
resources.why_essential: Key approach for pineal region and allows gravity retraction of cerebellum.

resources.title: Interhemispheric Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Yasargil, Rhoton, Lawton)
resources.description: Midline approach through the interhemispheric fissure for parasagittal, falcine, and corpus callosum lesions. Anterior, middle, or posterior variants.
resources.positioning: Supine, head neutral or slightly flexed, may lateralize depending on target
resources.why_essential: Essential for midline deep lesions and transcallosal corridor.

resources.title: Bifrontal Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Bilateral frontal approach for large anterior skull base lesions, bilateral frontal pathology, and anterior communicating complex.
resources.positioning: Supine, head neutral, slight extension
resources.why_essential: Provides wide anterior skull base exposure for large tumors.

resources.title: Orbitozygomatic Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple (Sekhar, Al-Mefty, Zabramski)
resources.description: Extension of pterional approach with removal of orbital rim and/or zygomatic arch for enhanced anterior and middle skull base exposure.
resources.positioning: Supine, head rotated 30-60 degrees contralateral
resources.why_essential: Increases surgical freedom for deep skull base lesions.

resources.title: Transpetrosal Approach (Anterior and Posterior)
resources.author: Multiple (Kawase, Al-Mefty, Sekhar)
resources.description: Approaches through the petrous bone. Anterior (Kawase) drills the petrous apex; posterior involves presigmoid/retrolabyrinthine corridor.
resources.positioning: Supine, head turned contralateral 60-90 degrees
resources.why_essential: Critical for petroclival region pathology requiring wide exposure.

resources.title: Far Lateral (Extreme Lateral) Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Heros, Spetzler, Bertalanffy)
resources.description: Posterolateral approach to the ventral foramen magnum region, lower clivus, and anterior brainstem with or without C1 arch removal.
resources.positioning: Lateral/park bench or prone with head turned, hockey stick incision
resources.why_essential: Only adequate approach for ventral foramen magnum pathology.

resources.title: Endoscopic Endonasal Transsphenoidal Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Kassam, Carrau, Cappabianca, Laws)
resources.description: Endoscopic approach through nasal corridor to sella, suprasellar, and extended to anterior skull base, clivus, and petrous apex.
resources.positioning: Supine, head in horseshoe or Mayfield, slight extension
resources.why_essential: Rapidly expanding approach for ventral skull base pathology.

resources.title: Temporal/Subtemporal Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Drake, Yasargil, Rhoton)
resources.description: Approach via temporal craniotomy with elevation of temporal lobe for access to middle fossa floor, tentorial incisura, and interpeduncular cistern.
resources.positioning: Supine, head turned 90 degrees, vertex down
resources.why_essential: Direct approach to tentorial incisura and middle fossa floor.

resources.title: Telovelar Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Matsushima, Rhoton)
resources.description: Microsurgical approach through the cerebellomedullary fissure to access the fourth ventricle without splitting the vermis.
resources.positioning: Prone or sitting, neck flexed
resources.why_essential: Avoids vermian splitting for fourth ventricle access.

resources.title: Supraorbital Keyhole Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple (Perneczky, Reisch, Cohen-Gadol)
resources.description: Minimally invasive approach via eyebrow incision and small supraorbital craniotomy for anterior skull base and anterior circulation pathology.
resources.positioning: Supine, head extended 15-20 degrees, rotated 15-30 degrees contralateral
resources.why_essential: Growing role in minimally invasive cranial surgery.

resources.title: Translabyrinthine Approach
resources.author: Multiple (House, Brackmann)
resources.description: Lateral skull base approach through the labyrinth for CPA lesions when hearing is not serviceable.
resources.positioning: Supine, head turned contralateral, ear up
resources.why_essential: Provides excellent facial nerve identification for large VS.

resources.title: Middle Fossa Approach
resources.author: Multiple (House, Garcia-Ibanez)
resources.description: Extradural middle fossa approach for IAC and petrous apex with hearing preservation potential.
resources.positioning: Supine, head turned 90 degrees, temporal craniotomy
resources.why_essential: Best approach for hearing preservation in small intracanalicular tumors.

resources.title: Contralateral Interhemispheric Transfalcine Transprecuneus Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Lawton, Cohen-Gadol)
resources.description: Advanced approach through the contralateral interhemispheric fissure, across the falx, for medial parietal/occipital and atrial lesions.
resources.positioning: Lateral decubitus, lesion side down (gravity retraction)
resources.why_essential: Elegant gravity-assisted approach for deep medial lesions.

resources.title: Transnasal Transsphenoidal Approach (Microscopic)
resources.author: Multiple (Hardy, Laws, Ciric)
resources.description: Classic microscopic transsphenoidal approach to the sella via sublabial or endonasal route.
resources.positioning: Supine, head slightly extended, image guidance
resources.why_essential: Classic pituitary approach — foundation for endoscopic techniques.

resources.title: Transcallosal Approach
resources.author: Multiple (Apuzzo, Yasargil, Rhoton)
resources.description: Interhemispheric approach with callosotomy for access to the third ventricle, lateral ventricles, and deep midline structures.
resources.positioning: Supine, head neutral or slightly flexed
resources.why_essential: Primary open approach for third ventricle pathology.

resources.title: Parasagittal/Parafalcine Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Craniotomy crossing or adjacent to the superior sagittal sinus for parasagittal and falcine lesions.
resources.positioning: Supine, head neutral, body tilted for venous drainage
resources.why_essential: Critical approach with major venous considerations.

resources.title: Convexity Craniotomy
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Direct craniotomy over a convexity lesion. Size and shape tailored to the lesion with navigation.
resources.positioning: Variable — supine, lateral, or prone depending on lesion location
resources.why_essential: Most straightforward craniotomy — fundamentals of technique apply to all.

resources.title: Skull Clamp Placement Principles
resources.author: Multiple (Connolly, Mayfield manual)
resources.description: Principles of three-point skull fixation including pin placement zones, avoidance zones (thin bone, sinuses, sutures in pediatrics), and troubleshooting.
resources.why_essential: Every cranial case starts here. Bad clamp placement compromises the entire operation.

resources.title: Patient Positioning in Neurosurgery
resources.author: Multiple (Connolly, Gelb, Spetzler)
resources.description: Comprehensive guide to neurosurgical positioning: supine, prone, lateral, park bench, sitting, Concorde. Includes physiologic considerations, pressure point padding, and venous drainage optimization.

positions.supine: Standard for anterior/lateral cranial, transsphenoidal, anterior cervical
positions.prone: Posterior fossa midline, posterior cervical/thoracic/lumbar spine
positions.lateral: CPA, lateral posterior fossa, lateral cervical
positions.park_bench: CPA, posterior fossa lateral — variant of lateral with head flexion and rotation
positions.sitting: Posterior fossa — falling out of favor due to VAE risk
positions.concorde: Alternative to sitting — prone with head elevated

resources.why_essential: Position determines approach success. Must understand physiology and complications.

resources.title: Scalp Incision Planning
resources.author: Multiple (Yasargil, Rhoton, Cohen-Gadol)
resources.description: Principles of scalp incision design including blood supply considerations, cosmesis, muscle handling, and incision types (linear, curvilinear, question-mark, bicoronal, horseshoe).
resources.why_essential: Incision determines exposure and affects healing and cosmesis.

resources.title: Burr Hole Placement and Craniotomy Technique
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Principles of burr hole placement relative to key landmarks, dural tacking, craniotomy flap elevation, and bone flap management.
resources.why_essential: Technical foundation for every craniotomy.

resources.title: Dural Opening and Closure Techniques
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Techniques for dural opening (curvilinear, stellate, C-shaped), pedicled vs free dural flaps, and watertight closure methods.
resources.why_essential: Watertight closure prevents CSF leak — a major source of morbidity.

resources.title: Intraoperative Hemostasis Techniques
resources.author: Multiple
resources.description: Methods for achieving hemostasis including bipolar technique, hemostatic agents (Surgicel, Gelfoam, fibrin glue), bone wax, and venous sinus repair.
resources.why_essential: Hemostasis is the foundation of safe microsurgery.

resources.title: Microsurgical Dissection Principles
resources.author: Yasargil, Rhoton
resources.description: Core principles of microsurgical technique: arachnoid dissection, cisternal opening, vessel handling, tumor capsule dissection, and brain parenchyma management.
resources.why_essential: The art of neurosurgery lives in microsurgical technique.

resources.title: Intraoperative Neuromonitoring
resources.author: Multiple (Nuwer, Deletis)
resources.description: Principles and application of SSEPs, MEPs, EMG, BAER, and direct cortical/subcortical stimulation during neurosurgical procedures.
resources.why_essential: Monitoring guides safe resection and protects eloquent structures.

resources.title: Frameless Stereotactic Navigation
resources.author: Multiple (Medtronic StealthStation, Brainlab)
resources.description: Principles and use of image-guided navigation systems for surgical planning, trajectory planning, and intraoperative localization.
resources.why_essential: Standard of care for most cranial procedures.

resources.title: Intraoperative Ultrasound in Neurosurgery
resources.author: Multiple (Unsgaard, Selbekk)
resources.description: Use of intraoperative ultrasound for real-time lesion localization, resection control, and assessment of residual disease.
resources.why_essential: Real-time imaging that doesn't suffer from brain shift.

resources.title: Fluorescence-Guided Surgery (5-ALA, ICG, Fluorescein)
resources.author: Multiple (Stummer, Raabe)
resources.description: Use of fluorescent agents for tumor visualization (5-ALA for gliomas), vascular assessment (ICG), and blood-brain barrier disruption (fluorescein).
resources.why_essential: Improves extent of resection and vascular assessment.

resources.title: Awake Craniotomy Techniques
resources.author: Multiple (Berger, Duffau, Sanai)
resources.description: Techniques for awake craniotomy including patient selection, anesthetic management, cortical/subcortical mapping, and language/motor testing.
resources.why_essential: Essential for safe resection near eloquent cortex.

resources.title: Brain Retraction Alternatives
resources.author: Multiple (Cohen-Gadol, Yasargil)
resources.description: Modern approaches to minimize brain retraction: dynamic retraction, gravity-assisted positioning, CSF drainage, cisternal opening, and retractorless surgery.
resources.why_essential: Retraction injury is avoidable with proper technique and positioning.

resources.title: Intraoperative MRI
resources.author: Multiple (Black, Hall, Nimsky)
resources.description: Use of intraoperative MRI for real-time assessment of extent of resection, brain shift correction, and residual disease detection.
resources.why_essential: Maximizes extent of resection particularly for gliomas.

resources.title: EVD Placement Technique
resources.author: Multiple (Greenberg, Connolly)
resources.description: Step-by-step technique for external ventricular drain placement including Kocher's point, trajectory planning, and troubleshooting.
resources.why_essential: Core neurosurgical procedure performed frequently in emergencies.

resources.title: Aneurysm Surgery: Clipping Principles
resources.author: Lawton, Spetzler, Yasargil
resources.description: Principles of aneurysm clipping including proximal control, clip selection (straight, curved, fenestrated, stacked), temporary clipping, and aneurysm-specific strategies.
resources.why_essential: Core cerebrovascular skill with specific technical principles.

resources.title: AVM Grading and Surgical Planning
resources.author: Spetzler, Martin, Lawton
resources.description: Spetzler-Martin grading, supplementary grading, and surgical principles for AVM resection including circumferential dissection and deep feeder management.
resources.why_essential: Systematic approach to AVM surgery based on grading.

resources.title: EC-IC Bypass Techniques
resources.author: Lawton, Sekhar, Spetzler
resources.description: Extracranial-intracranial bypass techniques: STA-MCA direct bypass, radial artery/saphenous vein interposition graft, and high-flow bypass.
resources.why_essential: Critical skill for complex cerebrovascular surgery.

resources.title: Glioma Surgery Principles
resources.author: Berger, Sanai, Duffau
resources.description: Principles of glioma resection including maximal safe resection, functional boundaries, sulcal-gyral anatomy, white matter tract anatomy, and extent of resection impact on survival.
resources.why_essential: Defines modern neuro-oncologic surgical practice.

resources.title: Meningioma Classification and Surgical Strategies
resources.author: Al-Mefty, Sekhar, DeMonte
resources.description: WHO classification, Simpson grading, and location-specific surgical strategies for meningiomas (convexity, parasagittal, skull base, posterior fossa).
resources.why_essential: Meningiomas are the most common primary brain tumor requiring surgery.

resources.title: Vestibular Schwannoma Surgery
resources.author: Samii, Sekhar, Bambakidis
resources.description: Approach selection (retrosigmoid vs translabyrinthine vs middle fossa), facial nerve preservation strategies, and hearing preservation considerations.
resources.why_essential: High-stakes surgery where approach selection dramatically affects outcomes.

resources.title: Anterior Skull Base Tumor Surgery
resources.author: DeMonte, Al-Mefty, Hanna
resources.description: Surgical management of anterior skull base tumors including olfactory groove meningiomas, esthesioneuroblastomas, and anterior cranial base reconstruction.
resources.why_essential: Complex skull base surgery requiring multidisciplinary approach.

resources.title: Posterior Fossa Tumor Surgery
resources.author: Multiple (Samii, Sekhar, Cohen-Gadol)
resources.description: Surgical management of posterior fossa tumors including medulloblastoma, hemangioblastoma, ependymoma, and metastases.
resources.why_essential: Posterior fossa demands specialized approach and technique knowledge.

resources.title: Deep Brain Stimulation Technique
resources.author: Multiple (Lozano, Starr, Gross)
resources.description: DBS lead implantation technique including target selection (STN, GPi, VIM), stereotactic planning, microelectrode recording, and programming.
resources.why_essential: Growing functional neurosurgery subspecialty with expanding indications.

resources.title: Epilepsy Surgery
resources.author: Multiple (Spencer, Engel, Duckworth)
resources.description: Epilepsy surgery techniques including anterior temporal lobectomy, selective amygdalohippocampectomy, SEEG electrode placement, and laser ablation.
resources.why_essential: Specialized surgery requiring integration of electrophysiology and anatomy.

resources.title: Microvascular Decompression (MVD)
resources.author: Multiple (Jannetta, Barker)
resources.description: MVD technique for trigeminal neuralgia and hemifacial spasm including retrosigmoid approach, identification of offending vessel, and Teflon pledget placement.
resources.why_essential: Common functional procedure with excellent outcomes when technique is sound.

resources.title: Chiari Malformation Decompression
resources.author: Multiple (Milhorat, Tubbs)
resources.description: Posterior fossa decompression for Chiari I malformation including bone-only vs duraplasty, tonsillar management, and syrinx considerations.
resources.why_essential: Common pediatric and adult neurosurgical procedure.

resources.title: ACDF (Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion)
resources.author: Multiple (Smith-Robinson, Cloward, Benzel)
resources.description: Step-by-step ACDF technique including anterior cervical approach, discectomy, endplate preparation, graft/cage selection, and plating.
resources.why_essential: One of the most commonly performed spine procedures.

resources.title: Posterior Cervical Laminectomy and Fusion
resources.author: Multiple (Heller, Anderson, Benzel)
resources.description: Posterior cervical decompression and instrumented fusion including lateral mass screws, pedicle screws, and laminoplasty alternatives.
resources.why_essential: Essential spine procedure for multilevel cervical pathology.

resources.title: Lumbar Microdiscectomy
resources.author: Multiple (Caspar, Yasargil, McCulloch)
resources.description: Microsurgical technique for lumbar disc herniation including patient positioning, approach, disc fragment removal, and nerve root decompression.
resources.why_essential: Bread-and-butter spine procedure with high patient satisfaction.

resources.title: TLIF/PLIF (Transforaminal/Posterior Lumbar Interbody Fusion)
resources.author: Multiple (Harms, Cloward, Benzel)
resources.description: Interbody fusion techniques via posterior approach including cage placement, pedicle screw instrumentation, and graft options.
resources.why_essential: Core spine fusion procedure for degenerative disease.

resources.title: Lateral Interbody Fusion (XLIF/OLIF/DLIF)
resources.author: Multiple (Ozgur, Pimenta)
resources.description: Lateral retroperitoneal approach to the lumbar spine for interbody fusion, including neuromonitoring and psoas management.
resources.why_essential: Increasingly common minimally invasive spine approach.

resources.title: Intradural Spinal Tumor Surgery
resources.author: Multiple (McCormick, Klekamp)
resources.description: Surgical technique for intradural extramedullary (meningioma, schwannoma) and intramedullary (ependymoma, astrocytoma) spinal tumors.
resources.why_essential: Technically demanding surgery requiring precise microsurgical technique.

resources.title: VP Shunt Placement
resources.author: Multiple (Greenberg, Connolly)
resources.description: Ventriculoperitoneal shunt placement technique including ventricular catheter placement, valve selection, tunneling, and peritoneal insertion.
resources.why_essential: Fundamental neurosurgical procedure for hydrocephalus.

resources.title: Endoscopic Third Ventriculostomy (ETV)
resources.author: Multiple (Warf, Hopf)
resources.description: Endoscopic technique for creating a stoma in the floor of the third ventricle for obstructive hydrocephalus treatment.
resources.why_essential: Shunt-independent treatment for obstructive hydrocephalus.

resources.title: Operative Neurosurgery (Journal)
resources.author: CNS / Oxford Academic
resources.description: Peer-reviewed journal focused specifically on operative neurosurgical technique with detailed step-by-step articles.
resources.why_essential: Best journal specifically focused on operative technique.

resources.title: Journal of Neurosurgery
resources.author: AANS / JNS Publishing
resources.description: Premier neurosurgery journal covering all subspecialties with clinical research, case reports, and technique articles.
resources.why_essential: The flagship neurosurgery journal.

resources.title: Neurosurgery (Journal)
resources.author: CNS / Wolters Kluwer
resources.description: Major neurosurgery journal with clinical articles, operative videos, and consensus guidelines.
resources.why_essential: CNS official journal with excellent clinical content.

resources.title: World Neurosurgery
resources.author: Elsevier
resources.description: International neurosurgery journal with global perspective on techniques, case reports, and clinical research.
resources.why_essential: Broad coverage of global neurosurgical practice and technique.

resources.title: Neurosurgical Focus (Journal)
resources.author: AANS / JNS Publishing
resources.description: Topic-focused neurosurgery journal with each issue dedicated to a specific surgical topic or pathology.
resources.why_essential: Deep dives into specific neurosurgical topics — excellent for case prep.
